claim: Cyclin A2 destruction is necessary to switch from unstable to stable kt-mt attachments in mitosis.
evidence_sentences: The switch to more stable k-MT attachments in metaphase requires the proteasome-dependent destruction of cyclin A in prometaphase.
Persistent cyclin A expression prevents k-MT stabilization even in cells with aligned chromosomes.
By contrast, k-MTs are prematurely stabilized in cyclin-A-deficient cells.
